info.title: Keasy API
info.description: Keasy — data pipeline execution, cloud storage, and Gaia-X compliance API

get.description:
Returns the authenticated user's profile, org, and effective role.
Protected by session_required but NOT tenant_context_required.

Returns the list of dataspaces the authenticated user has access to,
resolved from the `dataspaces` session value to display info via
the dataspaces table. Used by the sidebar instance switcher.

400.description: Invalid connection or container not found

200.description: List of files in the connection

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Relative file path within the connection

400.description: Schema inference failed or unsupported file type

200.description: AI answer with optional SPARQL results
SSE stream of job progress events
List of users in the org

AskResultCode.description: Result code for AI ask responses — replaces raw string literals.

ComplyEvent.description: SSE event emitted during the comply pipeline.

index.description: Index 0-5, maps to frontend PHASES array

phase.description: Phase name: key_generation, certificate, lrn_request, signing, compliance_submission, complete

ComplyRequest.description: Request body for the one-click comply endpoint.

cert_chain_pem.description: Optional PEM cert chain — fallback if Caddy certs are unavailable.

ComplyResponse.description: Response from the one-click comply endpoint.

failed_phase.description: Which phase failed: key_generation, certificate, lrn_request, lp_signing, tc_signing, compliance_submission.

DataResponse_Value.description: Typed envelope for successful API responses: `{ "data": T }`.

JobEvent.description: SSE event emitted by the job runner at each execution phase.

JobRuntimeError.description:
Runtime job error — stored in the database as JSON on a failed job.
This is NOT an API error type; it is a serializable record of what went wrong during execution.

OrgMember.description:
An org member — a Keycloak user's membership in a Keasy organization.
Profile fields (email, first_name, last_name) are cached from OIDC tokens.
